title: Seems compat to see 'No such image' when creating container
body:
[ NO NEW TESTS NEEDED ]
Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@mscherer could you try this out and see if this fixes the problem.
@mheon @jwhonce The Utils.Error code seems like it could use some work.

It looks like the Docker clients are expecting "No such %s" in their error messages, but we tend to return

"%s not found", Should we generalize this for Docker compatibility.

So I tired. It doesn't work. however, if the patch is change to modify the "Message" key, as in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/12315#issuecomment-970514938 , it work

(then I hit another issue where docker normalize image name and I have no idea on how to fix that one without lowering security)
Lets close this in favour of #12318
